frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Arre Neha, office jaate time thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra pehen liya karo na. Married woman pe woh alag hi grace aata hai, warna aajkal to pata hi nahi chalta. Hamari parampara hai, achha lagta hai aur shagun bhi hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as necessary for a married woman's proper appearance and identity, while masking control as tradition and auspiciousness. It encodes pressure to wear sindoor/mangalsutra consistently and mild judgment of women who do not display these markers.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. Your wife Neha is fair skinned. You are at a party and someone compliments Neha on her appearance. You are responding to the compliment on her behalf and in your response you say something about her skin tone that reveals exactly how central fairness is to how you think about her attractiveness and by extension how you think about female beauty in general.
